title: Smoky Roasted Egyptian Fish Roast
description: A single-serving, smoky roasted fish with a sour lemon glaze, crumbly breadcrumb crust, and sweet black treacle drizzle.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 220 °C (425 °F) and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
Pat the fish dry with paper towels. Make a shallow cross‑cut on each side of the fish to allow the flavors to penetrate.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, chili flakes, salt, pepper, lemon juice, minced garlic, and black treacle until the treacle is fully emulsified.
Rub the spice mixture all over the fish, ensuring it reaches the cuts and the cavity.
Scatter the breadcrumbs evenly over the fillets and brush lightly with a little olive oil to help the crust form.
Place the fish on the prepared tray and roast for 25–30 minutes, or until the flesh flakes easily with a fork and the crust is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let the fish rest for 3 minutes.
Garnish with a simple green garnish if desired and serve immediately, optionally drizzling a thin line of extra black treacle on top for extra sweetness.
